topic: No guided on-ramp into a new genre
unmet_need: Staged, goal-based exploration that coaches you into a genre
example_quotes: I have ADHD and the firehose of new releases is completely overwhelming. Discovery is way too high-effort to keep up with.
As an aspirational explorer I save tons of new music and forget all of it. Retention and rotation are the real unmet need.
Honestly when a recommendation explains why a track fits my taste, I trust it so much more. The 'why' is everything.
Same here. The algorithm keeps recommending the same five artists over and over. It feels like an echo chamber, not discovery.
Discovery is a one shot thing. There's no sense of progress, no goal, no feeling that I'm actually growing my taste over time.
New music never sticks for me. I play a recommended song once and forget it it never makes it into my rotation.
Busy parents need zero-effort discovery that respects their time and comes to them instead of making them dig.
Honestly I listen to the same 50 songs on repeat. I want new music but I have zero energy to go looking for it after work and kids.

topic: Stuck in a listening loop / same songs on repeat
unmet_need: Gentle nudges that expand the rotation without losing comfort
research_question: Q4 repeat-listening of the same content
example_quotes: The same 50 songs on repeat problem is so real. I love them but I'm in a loop and the app does nothing to gently expand it.
My problem is retention not discovery. I find cool new artists constantly, I just never build a lasting habit around any of them.
Your Episodes, Remove finished episodes after playing doesn't work. Thanks for the script recommendation, going to give that a try
Trying to get into classical as a total beginner and Spotify is no help. I need a beginner friendly path, not a firehose of 500 composers.
Stuck on repeat with the same comfort songs. The app never gently nudges me to expand my rotation.
Why does every recommended playlist sound the same as my current rotation? I feel stuck in a rut and the app is not helping me break out of it.
I want to break out of my rut but every recommendation sounds exactly like my current rotation. The app keeps me stuck instead of helping me grow.
Honestly the discovery features are fine for finding a song, terrible at turning it into a habit. I need the new artist to actually enter my rotation.

unmet_need: A comfort-to-curiosity control over how far recommendations stretch
example_quotes: A comfort-to-curiosity dial is genius. The biggest gap in every recommender is control over how far the recs stretch.
DJ sucks. Love the recommendation algorithm in "DJ", but hate the ai dj. Would love it if we had the option to turn him off
Power digger here. I want deep cuts and obscure artists below a certain popularity, but the algorithm plays it way too safe.
The algorithm is too cautious. I'd love a slider for how adventurous the recommendations are, from safe to totally out there.
I just want a control slider comfort versus curiosity instead of a fixed algorithm deciding how adventurous I get.
Grounded 'why this' bridges build trust. Recommendations without a reason feel random and I never act on them.
stuck in a rut listening to the same songs on repeat and the algorithm refuses to break me out of the loop
give me a dial for how adventurous my recs are. some days i want comfort, some days i want a real stretch

topic: Discovery surfaces don't lead anywhere new
unmet_need: Discovery that introduces artists you'd never have found yourself
example_quotes: I'd love a guided on-ramp to get into classical. Stage it for me week by week instead of one giant intimidating playlist.
discover weekly used to find me artists i'd never have found myself. now it's all safe picks i already know. discovery is broken
I open Discover Weekly, save a couple songs, and forget them by next week. High intent, nothing sticks. Story of my Spotify life.
spotify discover weekly is just my own playlist read back to me at this point. same artists every single week, total echo chamber
This is exactly my problem Discover Weekly recycles the familiar. It's an echo chamber of the same five artists I already follow.
My discover weekly is stale. Every week it's the same vibe and the same handful of artists. I'm bored of it.
Discover Weekly keeps recommending the same five artists every single week. It's an echo chamber and it never feels like real discovery.
I wish there was a way to commit to getting into one new genre and have the app coach me through it week by week with progress I can see.
